glossary.def: Resuming a base model's self-supervised next-token objective on a large corpus of in-domain raw text before any task fine-tuning. Domain-adaptive pre-training installs vocabulary and distributional knowledge the original pre-training never built; the cheaper task-adaptive variant (TAPT) pre-trains on the unlabeled task data itself.

Excluding a block's parameters from the optimizer so they keep their pre-trained values and receive no gradient, no optimizer state, and no backward pass. Freezing the lower (generic) blocks cuts trainable parameters and training memory and limits how far the weights can drift, which directly reduces catastrophic forgetting.

A schedule, introduced with ULMFiT, that begins with everything frozen but the head and thaws blocks one at a time from the top down, each given a smaller learning rate than the layer above it (discriminative fine-tuning). It lets the task-specific top adapt on stable features before the deeper, more general representations are relaxed.

The tendency of a network trained sequentially on a new task to overwrite the weights that encoded an earlier one, because the new loss is free to move in any direction that lowers it, including directions that wreck the old skill. It is the central obstacle to continual learning and the main risk of a domain fine-tune.

Folding a fraction of the model's original general-domain data back into each training batch so the old distribution's gradients stay alive. Even 1-10% replay recovers most retained capability for a small slowdown in adaptation, making it the most reliable and cheapest defense against forgetting.

A continual-learning regularizer that adds a quadratic penalty anchoring each weight to its old-task value, with stiffness set by that weight's Fisher-information importance. It approximates training on both tasks at once without keeping the old data, and matters most when the original corpus can no longer be revisited.

Reserved special tokens written inline into training sequences (a leading quality or domain marker) that let a model condition on provenance and be told at inference to imitate only the good data. They must be atomic tokenizer tokens, not plain-text strings, so the model cannot hallucinate them mid-generation.

The exponential of the entropy of the source mixture, exp(-sum p_i log p_i): the perplexity of the source distribution after dedup. It collapses toward 1 as one source dominates and rises toward the source count when the mix is even, so it measures how evenly tokens are spread, not how many sources were collected.

refs.cite: Gururangan, S., Marasovic, A., Swayamdipta, S. et al. (2020). Don't Stop Pretraining: Adapt Language Models to Domains and Tasks. ACL 2020.
Kirkpatrick, J., Pascanu, R., Rabinowitz, N. et al. (2017). Overcoming Catastrophic Forgetting in Neural Networks. PNAS 2017.
Howard, J. & Ruder, S. (2018). Universal Language Model Fine-tuning for Text Classification (ULMFiT). ACL 2018.
Bengio, Y., Louradour, J., Collobert, R. & Weston, J. (2009). Curriculum Learning. ICML 2009.
McCloskey, M. & Cohen, N. J. (1989). Catastrophic Interference in Connectionist Networks: The Sequential Learning Problem.
Chaudhry, A., Ranzato, M., Rohrbach, M. & Elhoseiny, M. (2019). Efficient Lifelong Learning with A-GEM. ICLR 2019.
Luo, Y., Yang, Z., Meng, F. et al. (2023). An Empirical Study of Catastrophic Forgetting in LLMs During Continual Fine-tuning.
